preamble: The shift from the voiceless bilabial fricative [ɸ] (phi) to the voiceless labiodental fricative [f] is a form of fronting and consonantalization. This change is extremely common when speakers adapt bilabial fricatives into languages where only labiodental fricatives exist.

examples.note: Early Greek [pʰ] became [ɸ] and was adapted into Latin as [f].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
